description.p: NetScope is a real-time network bandwidth monitor for Linux with a PyQt6 GUI. It shows per-process upload and download rates, active TCP/UDP connections, remote destinations with GeoIP country identification, and historical bandwidth graphs.

ul.li: Connection tracking — TCP/UDP with remote service identification
GeoIP — country flags next to remote hosts
Historical graphs — bandwidth over time stored in SQLite
Data cap tracking — monthly cap with warnings and projection
Custom alert rules — user-defined thresholds with desktop notifications
Traffic blocking — block a process via iptables with one click
Speed test — integrated network speed test
System tray with live bandwidth rates
